content.full_text:
City of Berkeley
Office of the City Attorney
Martin Luther King, Jr.
Civic Center Building
2180 Milvia Street
Berkeley, California 94704
Telecommunications Device for the Deaf (415) 644-6915

Telephone: (415) 644-6380
Telecopy: (415) 644-8641


August 2, 1990


Legal Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Colleague:

Several of our elected officers have asked for guidance in using
surplus campaign funds for their November 1990 re-election bid.
We would be grateful if you could advise us concerning the
following questions:

1)    Under the preliminary injunction issued in SEIU v FPPC, are
      there any conditions under which a candidate cannot use
      surplus campaign funds?

2)    If the Judge lifts the Preliminary Injunction, what, if any
      action, will the FPPC take against candidates who have
      transferred surplus funds to current campaigns?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Sincerely,

MANUELA ALBUQUERQUE
City Attorney

[signature]

SARAH REYNOSO
Deputy City Attorney

SR/lh

sections.question:
1) Under the preliminary injunction issued in SEIU v FPPC, are there any conditions under which a candidate cannot use surplus campaign funds?
2) If the Judge lifts the Preliminary Injunction, what, if any action, will the FPPC take against candidates who have transferred surplus funds to current campaigns?
sections.question_synthetic: The City of Berkeley seeks FPPC guidance on whether candidates can use surplus campaign funds for re-election campaigns under the SEIU v FPPC preliminary injunction, and what enforcement actions the FPPC may take if the injunction is lifted.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the City of Berkeley to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. Contains two numbered questions regarding campaign fund use and enforcement. No CONCLUSION or ANSWER section present as this is a request for advice, not an FPPC response letter.

2) If the Judge lifts the Preliminary Injunction, what, if any action, will the FPPC take against candidates who have transferred surplus funds to current campaigns?
